title: Slow-cooker vegetable curry
subtitle: Root vegetables and chickpeas in coconut milk
excerpt: Sweet potato, potato, butternut squash, and chickpeas slow-cooked in coconut milk with curry powder, turmeric, and garam masala for five to six hours. A thick, warming curry that improves overnight. Serve with basmati rice and flatbread.
sourceNotes: Vegetable curry with coconut milk became a British weeknight standard as plant-based cooking grew in popularity through the 2010s. The slow cooker version suits root vegetables especially well — they hold their shape over a long cook better than leafy vegetables and absorb the spiced coconut milk gradually.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freezes well. The vegetables soften further on reheating.

content.text: Cut root vegetables into large pieces — at least 3 cm. Smaller pieces will dissolve into the sauce over five to six hours rather than remaining as distinct vegetable pieces. Butternut squash in particular breaks down fast; keep the chunks generous.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a pan over medium heat. Fry the {{onion}} for 4 minutes. Add the {{garlic}}, {{ginger-root}}, {{curry-powder}}, and {{turmeric}} and stir for 1 minute. Transfer to the slow cooker. Add the {{sweet-potato}}, {{butternut-squash}}, {{chickpeas-tinned}}, {{tinned-tomatoes}}, {{coconut-milk}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Cook on low for 5 to 6 hours, until the vegetables are tender. Stir in the {{garam-masala}} and {{spinach-frozen}} if using. Cover and wait 5 minutes for the spinach to wilt. Taste and adjust seasoning.

items.symptom: Vegetables have dissolved into the sauce
items.cause: Cut too small, or cooked longer than 6 hours
items.fix: For next time, cut all vegetables into pieces at least 3 cm. Butternut squash especially needs to be kept large — it breaks down quickly

items.cause: Full-fat coconut milk and starchy sweet potato have thickened the sauce significantly
items.fix: Add 100 ml vegetable stock or water and stir to loosen. A squeeze of lime also cuts through the richness

items.cause: Spices added raw rather than bloomed first
items.fix: Always fry the curry powder and turmeric in oil with the onion before adding to the slow cooker

content.text: Vegetable curry with coconut milk is a reliable plant-based slow-cooker dinner. The coconut milk provides body and richness without any dairy. It keeps for three days in the fridge and the flavour improves each day. Serve over basmati rice with flatbread and mango chutney.
